title: e2e: GetSafeIPAddress(): discourage its use
body:
Add header comment suggesting podman network create instead.

Stop using it in checkpoint tests. Turned out to be much more
complicated than expected.

And, remove obsolete f28-and-earlier code.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: FWIW I've been testing this in my no-retries PR. Saw [one flake](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4630379487821824/html/int-remote-fedora-37-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-with-file-locks--1) that I'm 90% sure is a bug in the test itself, the usual race condition with `-d` and `container has actually started doing what we want it to do` (in this case, run `flock`). If anyone believes this to be a real bug with my new approach, please speak now.
